title: Olivia Dean wins Best New Artist Grammy, becoming 9th female winner in a row: 'I'm a product of bravery'
text: Olivia Dean, a British singer-songwriter, claimed the Best New Artist Grammy at the 2026 ceremony, extending a remarkable streak in the category. Her victory marks the ninth consecutive win by a solo female artist, continuing a run that began in 2018 with Alessia Cara and includes subsequent winners like Dua Lipa, Billie Eilish, Megan Thee Stallion, Olivia Rodrigo, Samara Joy, Victoria Monét, and Chappell Roan. During her acceptance speech, Dean stated: I'm up here as the granddaughter of an immigrant. I'm a product of bravery and I think those people need to be celebrated. Dean entered the ceremony as the frontrunner, powered by her breakout hit Man I Need, which reached No. 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 and dominated pop radio. Her momentum, combined with critical acclaim for her album The Art of Loving, made her the standout competitor in the field.
